title: CLAUDE.md — dctools
text: file guides Claude Code working repository. dctools personal R package tested helper functions prediction- causal-inference research. repo package named dctools.

what: Design philosophy (read before adding or changing functions) — IMPORTANT
title: CLAUDE.md — dctools
text: dctools one half system. half biostat-support skills marketplace (separate repo). division labor hard contract: dctools = mechanics. Tested, documented functions repetitive work (intake QC, validation, diagnostics, scaffolding, formatting). opinion call . Skills = judgment. , , check, red flags. lives marketplace, . Therefore, extending dctools: Add function ’s reusable mechanic home skill call (clear place ROADMAP.md). Don’t add one-, project-specific logic. Keep judgment/caveats function — paragraph “/” belongs skill rule, buried roxygen. Roxygen documents , use . find wanting hand-write mechanic inside skill analysis, belongs instead. ROADMAP.md lists planned functions grouped analysis stage; group maps home skill (data-preparation, analysis-design, statistical-critique, simulation-study, prediction-eval, …). Keep ROADMAP.md marketplace’s docs/design-philosophy.md sync (doc holds full complementarity map). Build order demand-driven, roadmap order (decision 2026-07-04, see memos/decisions.md): ROADMAP sections 9-12 verification gates make agent-delegated analyses auditable — implement function real analysis home skill needs gate. Cross-repo strategy: biostat-support/docs/north-star.md. Current focus, next actions, progress live PROJECT.md — refresh (/project-status) change. Tooling friction goes workflow-feedback.md, decision log.

title: CLAUDE.md — dctools
text: DESCRIPTION: Authors@R (redundant Author:/Maintainer:); MIT license. Exports: every exported function roxygen block (@param, @return, @export) testthat test. Internal helpers prefixed . exported. Imports: call imported functions fully qualified (pkg::fn) NAMESPACE stays export-. Keep Imports minimal; heavy/optional packages go Suggests guarded requireNamespace(). ASCII-R sources (R CMD check ---cran errors non-ASCII; use \uxxxx plain ASCII — em-dashes/accents R/). roxygen/export change: run devtools::document() (regenerates man/ + NAMESPACE), devtools::test() devtools::check() — must pass clean. CI runs R-CMD-check + coverage + pkgdown errors warnings. Format Air (air format .; config air.toml).

title: CLAUDE.md — dctools
text: Data manipulation: dplyr surface grammar; arrow/dbplyr large data (verbs); data.table profiled hot paths. Naming: snake_case vars/columns; verb_noun() functions; .dot_prefixed internals. PHI: never code, tests, examples, fixtures.

title: CLAUDE.md — dctools
text: man/ roxygen-generated; hand-edit (committed CI can build).
code: devtools::document()   # after roxygen changes — updates man/ + NAMESPACE devtools::test() devtools::check()      # must pass clean

title: Contributing to dctools
text: personal toolkit, follows package discipline stays maintainable trustworthy.

title: Contributing to dctools
text: Branch main — never commit directly main. Add change code R/, Roxygen2 block (@param, @return, @export) every exported function. Internal helpers prefixed .. Add testthat test tests/testthat/ new behavior. Regenerate docs check: Format Air: air format . Update NEWS.md, open PR. CI (R-CMD-check, coverage) must green.
code: devtools::document()   # updates NAMESPACE + man/ devtools::test() devtools::check()      # must pass clean

title: Contributing to dctools
text: Data manipulation: dplyr surface grammar. large data, use verbs arrow dbplyr backends. Reserve data.table profiled hot paths . Dependencies: keep Imports minimal; put heavy optional packages Suggests guard requireNamespace(). PHI ever enters repo, including tests examples.

text: R/ + tests/testthat/ - package source tests; devtools::check() must pass clean. ROADMAP.md - planned functions grouped analysis stage, home skill. memos/decisions.md - decision log; workflow-feedback.md - tooling friction.

title: dctools roadmap
text: Repeated data-science tasks package functions. [x] = shipped, [ ] = planned. Grouped analysis-pipeline stage. Conventions: dplyr surface grammar; arrow/dbplyr large data; base R keeps dependencies low.

title: dctools roadmap
text: check_types() — text--numeric/date, logical--text, high-cardinality, binary--numeric check_colnames() / clean_colnames() — non-syntactic, dupes, case-collisions, spaces refactor clean_colnames() wrap janitor::make_clean_names() (robust hand-rolled gsub chain) keeping colname_map /attribute; check_colnames() stays custom (report-). janitor Suggests, guarded (errors absent) - see memos/decisions.md 2026-07-10. coerce_types(data, spec) — apply declarative/codebook type spec, report coercions + NAs introduced read_any(path) — extension dispatch (csv/xlsx/sas7bdat/dta/parquet/rds) + encoding/locale guard

title: dctools roadmap
text: detect_missing_sentinels() — disguised-missing numeric codes & string tokens convert_sentinels(data, rules) — apply confirmed sentinels -> NA /audit missingness_report(data, ) — per-column %, co-occurrence, MCAR/MAR signal plot_missingness(data) — vis_miss-style figure theme_dc()

title: dctools roadmap
text: assert_columns(), check_unique_id(), flag_out_of_range() check_constant_cols() — zero-variance / -NA describe_cohort() — one-call structured QC report (diff across data refreshes) validate_schema(data, spec) — codebook-driven: columns + types + ranges + key uniqueness check_duplicate_cols(data) — identical content different name join_audit(left, right, , relationship) — strict-join wrapper (sets relationship/unmatched/na_matches) + /row-count match-rate audit trail; home skill: data-qc (references/cohort.md §2; external-mining ledger #1) check_id_consistency(data, id, time) — panel: ragged IDs, dup (id,time), gaps, non-monotonic check_dates(data) — implausible/future dates, end--start, DOB/age mismatch

title: dctools roadmap
text: check_collinearity() — numeric correlation pairs (first pass) extend check_collinearity() — Cramer’s V (categorical), VIF, perfect-alias detection near_zero_var(data) — caret-style near-constant predictors (caret dependency) correlation_heatmap(data) — themed, clustered

title: dctools roadmap
text: winsorize() / clamp() — cap percentiles clinical bounds, count affected standardize_factors(data) — trim/case-normalize/collapse rare levels, report level map recode_from_dict(data, dict) — apply value-label dictionary (Stata/SAS-style) unit_convert() — clinical conversions registry (creatinine, weight, temperature) dedupe(data, id, keep) — principled de-duplication kept/dropped log

title: dctools roadmap
text: make_binary() / make_surv() / make_composite() — consistent outcome construction make_splits(data, strata, prop|folds, seed) — stratified split/CV folds leakage guard build_recipe_skeleton() — tidymodels/recipes starting point codebook

title: dctools roadmap
text: theme_dc(), tbl1(), suppress_small_cells() plot_roc(), plot_calibration(), plot_decision_curve(), plot_forest() (generalize panel_diagnostics.R) safe_write(x, path) — refuse write individual-level data outside data/; auto-suppress small cells

title: dctools roadmap
text: session_fingerprint() — R version, package versions, seed, git SHA output/ tar_template() helpers — common targets patterns (map--outcomes, CV branching) compare_targets(results, targets, tolerances) — replication-target verification: per-target diff vs documented tolerance, PASS/FAIL table; home skill: estimation-diagnostics (references/replication.md; external-mining ledger #22). audit-reproducibility skill consumes comparison manuscript claims (#23)

title: dctools roadmap
text: check_residuals(model) — normality (QQ + Shapiro/KS), homoscedasticity (Breusch-Pagan, scale-location), residual-vs-fitted, autocorrelation (Durbin-Watson) check_influence(model) — Cook’s distance, leverage, DFFITS, outlier flags check_multicollinearity(model) — VIF / generalized VIF fitted model check_linearity(model) — partial-residual / component+residual checks continuous terms check_overdispersion(model) — Poisson/NB dispersion ratio + test check_separation(model) — (quasi-)complete separation logistic models model_assumption_report(model) — one-call bundle (guarded performance/see)

title: dctools roadmap
text: check_positivity(data, treatment, covariates) — propensity overlap: PS distribution arm, % near 0/1, suggested trimming, effective sample size check_balance(data, treatment, covariates, weights = NULL) — standardized mean differences pre/post weighting + Love plot check_weights(weights) — IPW diagnostics: max weight, ESS, coefficient variation, share total weight top-k check_eic_convergence(eic, n, se, threshold = NULL) — TMLE: |mean(EIC)| vs se/sqrt(n); flag non-convergence (default threshold se/(sqrt(n)*log(n))) check_ps_calibration(ps, treatment) — propensity model calibrated? summarize_eic(eic) — plug-vs one-step, influence-curve-based SE CIs

title: dctools roadmap
text: eval_discrimination(pred, obs) — AUC (+ DeLong CI), AUPRC, Brier eval_calibration(pred, obs) — calibration slope/intercept, ICI / E50 / E90, calibration curve eval_clinical_utility(pred, obs) — decision-curve / net-benefit across thresholds bootstrap_optimism(fit_fun, data) — Harrell optimism-corrected performance + shrinkage factor crossfit_performance(...) — honest CV / cross-fit metrics CIs (leakage-guarded splits) compare_auc(pred1, pred2, obs) — DeLong paired test (NRI/IDI caveats)

title: dctools roadmap
text: coverage_report(estimates, ses, truth) — bias, RMSE, CI coverage, Monte-Carlo SE (feeds simulation-study skill) summarize_sim(results) — tidy aggregation estimator runs scenario check_ci_coverage(ci_lo, ci_hi, truth) — nominal vs empirical coverage MC error bars

what: Reusable Utilities and Project Scaffolding for Biostatistics Research

text: dctools personal toolkit repeated-task helpers prediction- causal-inference research R. package lives helpers repository (package named dctools).

title: Reusable Utilities and Project Scaffolding for Biostatistics Research
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("imbroglio-dc/helpers")

title: Reusable Utilities and Project Scaffolding for Biostatistics Research
text: create_project() single entry point project archetypes:
code: # Analysis project: clones the analysis template, stamps the name, # optionally adds a targets pipeline. dctools::create_project("sepsis-aki", type = "analysis", targets = TRUE)  # R package: wraps usethis::create_package() and layers the best-practice set # (MIT license, testthat 3, roxygen markdown, pkgdown, CI, NEWS, .claude/). dctools::create_project("mypkg", type = "package")

title: Reusable Utilities and Project Scaffolding for Biostatistics Research
text: Data manipulation uses dplyr surface grammar; large data verbs run arrow dbplyr backends. data.table reserved profiled hot paths. See CONTRIBUTING.md. Note: man/ documentation generated devtools::document(). cloning, run devtools::document() R CMD check.

what: Assert that required columns are present — assert_columns
title: Assert that required columns are present — assert_columns
text: Errors (via cli::cli_abort()) cols missing data, naming offenders. Invisibly returns data can used inline pipe.

title: Assert that required columns are present — assert_columns
code: assert_columns(data, cols)

title: Assert that required columns are present — assert_columns
text: data data frame. cols Character vector required column names.

title: Assert that required columns are present — assert_columns
text: data, invisibly.

title: Assert that required columns are present — assert_columns
code: df <- data.frame(id = 1:3, age = c(40, 50, 60)) assert_columns(df, c("id", "age"))

what: Find highly correlated numeric column pairs — check_collinearity
title: Find highly correlated numeric column pairs — check_collinearity
text: Pairwise Pearson correlation across numeric columns, returning pairs threshold absolute value, sorted strength. first-pass redundancy screen (extend VIF / Cramer's V needed).

title: Find highly correlated numeric column pairs — check_collinearity
code: check_collinearity(data, threshold = 0.9)

title: Find highly correlated numeric column pairs — check_collinearity
text: data data frame. threshold Absolute correlation cutoff (default 0.9).

title: Find highly correlated numeric column pairs — check_collinearity
text: data frame: col1, col2, correlation, sorted abs(). Empty fewer two numeric columns pair qualifies.

title: Flag problematic column names — check_colnames
text: Reports columns whose names empty, non-syntactic, contain whitespace leading/trailing spaces, contain non-ASCII characters, duplicated, collide another name case.

title: Flag problematic column names — check_colnames
code: check_colnames(data)

title: Flag problematic column names — check_colnames
text: data data frame.

title: Flag problematic column names — check_colnames
text: data frame column comma-separated issues string, one row per problematic name (empty names clean).

what: Identify constant or all-missing columns — check_constant_cols
title: Identify constant or all-missing columns — check_constant_cols
text: Identify constant -missing columns

title: Identify constant or all-missing columns — check_constant_cols
code: check_constant_cols(data)

title: Identify constant or all-missing columns — check_constant_cols
text: data data frame.

title: Identify constant or all-missing columns — check_constant_cols
text: data frame: column, issue ("missing" "constant"), n_unique (non-missing unique values). Empty none.

what: Heuristically flag likely column-type problems — check_types
title: Heuristically flag likely column-type problems — check_types
text: Conservative checks surface common typing issues review: text parses numeric dates, two-valued text really logical, high-cardinality text, numeric columns really binary.

title: Heuristically flag likely column-type problems — check_types
code: check_types(data, max_factor_levels = 50)

title: Heuristically flag likely column-type problems — check_types
text: data data frame. max_factor_levels many unique values, text flagged high-cardinality (default 50).

title: Heuristically flag likely column-type problems — check_types
text: data frame: column, stored_type, issue, detail (one row per finding; empty nothing flagged).

what: Check that a column (or set of columns) uniquely identifies rows — check_unique_id
title: Check that a column (or set of columns) uniquely identifies rows — check_unique_id
text: Check column (set columns) uniquely identifies rows

title: Check that a column (or set of columns) uniquely identifies rows — check_unique_id
code: check_unique_id(data, id, error = TRUE)

title: Check that a column (or set of columns) uniquely identifies rows — check_unique_id
text: data data frame. id Character vector key column name(s). error Logical; TRUE (default) abort duplicates, otherwise warn return duplicated keys.

title: Check that a column (or set of columns) uniquely identifies rows — check_unique_id
text: Invisibly, data frame duplicated keys counts (empty key unique).

title: Check that a column (or set of columns) uniquely identifies rows — check_unique_id
code: df <- data.frame(id = c(1, 1, 2), x = 1:3) check_unique_id(df, "id", error = FALSE) #> Warning: `id` ("id") does not uniquely identify rows: 1 duplicated key.

what: Configure a local crew parallel controller — configure_parallel
title: Configure a local crew parallel controller — configure_parallel
text: Creates crew::crew_controller_local() capped requested worker count available system RAM divided memory_per_worker, pipeline oversubscribe memory. Requires crew ps packages.

title: Configure a local crew parallel controller — configure_parallel
code: configure_parallel(   max_workers = parallel::detectCores() - 1,   memory_per_worker = 4,   name = "dctools_local" )

title: Configure a local crew parallel controller — configure_parallel
text: max_workers Maximum workers spawn (default detectCores() - 1). memory_per_worker Target RAM per worker, GB (default 4). name Controller name (default "dctools_local").

title: Configure a local crew parallel controller — configure_parallel
text: crew_controller_local object use targets pipeline.

what: Scaffold a new research project — create_project
title: Scaffold a new research project — create_project
text: Creates project one two archetypes: "analysis" - builds code/ + data/ + output/ skeleton via make_file_dirs() writes project mechanics directly (PHI-aware .gitignore, renv-activation .Rprofile, <name>.Rproj, README stub, memos/ + workflow-feedback.md state stack, optional targets pipeline stub). writes CLAUDE.md .claude/: router generated project-setup skill safety hooks live installed kernel (~/.claude/), per-project copy drift. PHI protection must travel repo lives .gitignore (raw/derived data data extensions ignored). "package" - wraps usethis::create_package() layers package best-practices set (MIT license, testthat 3, roxygen markdown, pkgdown, R-CMD-check + coverage + pkgdown GitHub Actions, NEWS, self-contained .claude/).

title: Scaffold a new research project — create_project
code: create_project(   name,   path = ".",   type = c("analysis", "package"),   targets = FALSE,   git = TRUE )

title: Scaffold a new research project — create_project
text: name Project name (also new directory name). path Parent directory create project (default "."). type "analysis" "package". targets Logical; analysis projects, scaffold _targets.R stub. git Logical; initialise fresh git repository (default TRUE).

title: Scaffold a new research project — create_project
text: created project path, invisibly.

what: dctools: Reusable Utilities and Project Scaffolding for Biostatistics Research — dctools-package
title: dctools: Reusable Utilities and Project Scaffolding for Biostatistics Research — dctools-package
text: personal toolkit repeated-task helpers prediction- causal-inference research R: project scaffolding (create_project()), package parallel-controller management, data validation, PHI-safe output handling, clinical formulas, house plotting/table defaults. Data manipulation follows dplyr-first grammar; large-data backends (arrow, dbplyr) reuse verbs.

title: dctools: Reusable Utilities and Project Scaffolding for Biostatistics Research — dctools-package
text: Maintainer: David Chen david.chen1089@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: One-call cohort QC description — describe_cohort
text: Bundles per-column overview QC screens (check_colnames, check_types, check_constant_cols, detect_missing_sentinels, check_collinearity) single structured report. Suitable diffing across data refreshes.

title: One-call cohort QC description — describe_cohort
code: describe_cohort(data)

title: One-call cohort QC description — describe_cohort
text: data data frame.

title: One-call cohort QC description — describe_cohort
text: named list: dims, overview, colname_issues, type_issues, constant_cols, missing_sentinels, collinearity.

title: Detect disguised-missing sentinel values — detect_missing_sentinels
text: Scans values commonly encode missingness stored NA: numeric codes (e.g. -9, 999) string tokens (e.g. "", "N/", "unknown", whitespace-). Never modifies data; returns report can decide convert.

title: Detect disguised-missing sentinel values — detect_missing_sentinels
code: detect_missing_sentinels(   data,   numeric_codes = c(-9, -99, -999, -9999, 99, 999, 9999),   string_tokens = c("", "NA", "N/A", ".", "null", "none", "missing", "unknown", "-",     "--"),   ignore_case = TRUE )

title: Detect disguised-missing sentinel values — detect_missing_sentinels
text: data data frame. numeric_codes Numeric vector suspect codes. string_tokens Character vector suspect tokens (compared trimming whitespace). ignore_case Logical; case-insensitive token matching (default TRUE).

title: Detect disguised-missing sentinel values — detect_missing_sentinels
text: data frame one row per column/sentinel found: column, type, sentinel, n, prop.

what: Flag values outside an expected numeric range — flag_out_of_range
title: Flag values outside an expected numeric range — flag_out_of_range
text: Returns indices (values) x fall lo hi. Useful codebook range checks data cleaning. NA values ignored.

title: Flag values outside an expected numeric range — flag_out_of_range
code: flag_out_of_range(x, lo = -Inf, hi = Inf, label = NULL)

title: Flag values outside an expected numeric range — flag_out_of_range
text: x Numeric vector. lo, hi Inclusive lower/upper bounds. Use -Inf/Inf disable side. label Optional name messaging.

title: Flag values outside an expected numeric range — flag_out_of_range
text: Invisibly, data frame columns index value --range element (empty range).

title: Flag values outside an expected numeric range — flag_out_of_range
code: flag_out_of_range(c(5, 200, NA, -1), lo = 0, hi = 100, label = "sbp") #> Warning: 2 sbp outside [0, 100].

title: Load (and optionally install) a vector of R packages — load_packages

title: Load (and optionally install) a vector of R packages — load_packages
code: load_packages(pkgs, install = TRUE, repos = getOption("repos"))

title: Load (and optionally install) a vector of R packages — load_packages
text: Named logical vector: TRUE = attached, FALSE = failed.

what: Create the standard project directory skeleton — make_file_dirs
title: Create the standard project directory skeleton — make_file_dirs

title: Create the standard project directory skeleton — make_file_dirs
code: make_file_dirs(   path = ".",   code = TRUE,   data = TRUE,   output = TRUE,   docs = TRUE,   tests = TRUE,   extra = NULL )

text: path Project root (default current directory). code, data, output, docs, tests Logical; create subtree. extra Optional character vector additional relative dirs create.

title: Create the standard project directory skeleton — make_file_dirs
text: path, invisibly.

what: Read R package names from a plain-text file — read_packages
title: Read R package names from a plain-text file — read_packages
text: Lines beginning # // (optional whitespace) treated comments ignored, blank lines. Implemented base R carries dependencies.

title: Read R package names from a plain-text file — read_packages
code: read_packages(path)

title: Read R package names from a plain-text file — read_packages
text: path Path text file one package name per line.

text: character vector package names (de-duplicated, blanks).

title: Read R package names from a plain-text file — read_packages
code: tmp <- tempfile() writeLines(c("dplyr", "# a comment", "", "ggplot2"), tmp) read_packages(tmp) #> [1] "dplyr"   "ggplot2"

what: Suppress small cells in a summary table — suppress_small_cells
title: Suppress small cells in a summary table — suppress_small_cells
text: Replaces counts cols fall 1:(threshold - 1) marker, aggregate outputs re-identify individuals. Zero left untouched (true zero disclosive); use mask_zero = TRUE also blank zeros complementary suppression concern.

title: Suppress small cells in a summary table — suppress_small_cells
code: suppress_small_cells(   data,   cols = NULL,   threshold = 11,   marker = "<11",   mask_zero = FALSE )

title: Suppress small cells in a summary table — suppress_small_cells
text: data data frame aggregate counts (never individual-level data). cols Columns screen. Default: numeric columns. threshold Minimum safe cell count (default 11; cells < threshold > 0 suppressed). marker Replacement value suppressed cells (default "<11"). mask_zero Logical; also suppress exact zeros (default FALSE).

title: Suppress small cells in a summary table — suppress_small_cells
text: data suppressed cells replaced marker (affected columns become character).

title: Suppress small cells in a summary table — suppress_small_cells
code: tab <- data.frame(group = c("a", "b"), n = c(3, 42)) suppress_small_cells(tab, cols = "n") #>   group   n #> 1     a <11 #> 2     b  42

title: Descriptive
text: Thin wrapper around gtsummary::tbl_summary() applies project defaults (median IQR continuous, n (%) categorical) optionally adds overall column -group p-value. Requires gtsummary package.

title: Descriptive
code: tbl1(   data,   by = NULL,   include = NULL,   add_overall = !is.null(by),   add_p = FALSE )

title: Descriptive
text: data data frame. Optional grouping column, given string. include Optional character vector columns include (default: ). add_overall Logical; add overall column (default TRUE supplied). add_p Logical; add group comparison p-value (default FALSE).

title: Descriptive
text: gtsummary table object.

title: House ggplot2 theme — theme_dc
text: clean minimal theme used across project figures: minor grid, muted axis titles, left-aligned plot title.

title: House ggplot2 theme — theme_dc
code: theme_dc(base_size = 12, base_family = "")

title: House ggplot2 theme — theme_dc
text: base_size Base font size (default 12). base_family Base font family (default "").

title: House ggplot2 theme — theme_dc
text: ggplot2 theme object.

title: House ggplot2 theme — theme_dc
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() + theme_dc()

title: Workflow feedback - dctools
text: Tooling friction - devtools/R CMD check quirks, CI problems, formatting-tool issues. Kept distinct memos/decisions.md (decisions). Format: YYYY-MM-DD - <tool> - friction - -> action 2026-07-10 - air - air binary PATH environment; run air format . commit -> hand-matched surrounding style; install air (cargo/binary) restore format step.

title: dctools (development version)
text: Initial package scaffold (renamed helpers script collection). Project scaffolding: create_project() (analysis + package archetypes) make_file_dirs(). create_project(type = "analysis") now builds project directly instead cloning external template: PHI-aware .gitignore, .Rprofile, .Rproj, README stub, memos/ + workflow-feedback.md state stack. longer writes CLAUDE.md .claude/ — router generated project-setup skill safety hooks live installed kernel. Removes runtime network dependency (analysis archetype now unit-tested) template_repo argument. Package & environment helpers: read_packages(), load_packages(), configure_parallel(). Data validation: assert_columns(), check_unique_id(), flag_out_of_range(). Data-intake QC: describe_cohort(), detect_missing_sentinels(), check_colnames(), clean_colnames(), check_types(), check_constant_cols(), check_collinearity(). PHI-safe output: suppress_small_cells(). Clinical formulas: ckd_epi_2021(). House plotting / tables: theme_dc(), tbl1().
